title: rubus melanoxylon subsp. insolatus
intro: Rubus melanoxylon subsp. insolatus, also known as the Himalayan black raspberry, is a rare and lesser-known species of the Rubus genus, characterized by its dark purple fruit and medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus melanoxylon subsp. insolatus include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
